guide_review_response.work_item: v4 mid-stage handoff documentation quality review
guide_review_response.summary:
The v4 commit + schema fidelity + work-completion claims are all sound on disk (already ratified in prior turn). However, full-content review of v4 surfaces FIVE documentation defects -- two HIGH (g5-N numbering wrong; binding-ack count undercounted), three LOW/MED (cleaner.py undersells the g5-3 closure; f1 fix mischaracterized; canonical_param_pattern includes phantom decorator). The work itself is correct on disk; the v4 documentation has drift that would propagate to EA-5 if uncorrected.
Disposition: NO v4-amend commit (overhead + procedural noise). Instead: Guide-#6 MUST include a corrected binding-ack list (33+ acks, not v4's "27") + corrected g5-N numbering in the EA-5 init pointer. The v4 file persists as-is for audit (it shows what EA-4 documented at v4 emission time); the EA-5 init pointer authored by Guide-#6 becomes the authoritative restatement that EA-5 reads + restates verbatim in its iteration-1 gate. EA-5's iteration-1 gate then encodes the CORRECT ack chain forward.

defect.topic: g5-N numbering and content mapping is WRONG
defect.v4_claim: v4 lines 174-178 + 339-342: - g5-7 SATISFIED: "PS1 env-fallback default expression" - g5-8 SATISFIED: "PS1 alias attribute order" (FICTITIOUS -- no such ack exists) - g5-9 SATISFIED: "McpConfigPath derivation flows from $RepoRoot" - g5-10 FORWARD: STATUS append + A11
defect.actual_per_guide_5_dispositions: - g5-7 = duplicate test cleanup (delete lines 76-94 of test_vikunja_client_scope.py) - g5-8 = env-fallback default in 6 PS1 scripts - g5-9 = $McpConfigPath derivation in 2 scripts - g5-10 = A11 documented deviation commitment Source: 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit block.
defect.impact: The g5-7 duplicate test cleanup is omitted from v4's correction history entirely. EA-4 conflated env-fallback into g5-7 + invented "alias attribute order" as g5-8. If EA-5 restates v4's incorrect mapping in iteration-1 gate, the audit chain is broken.
defect.required_correction_for_ea_5_init_pointe: Guide-#6 MUST restate the CORRECT g5-7/g5-8/g5-9/g5-10 mapping in the EA-5 init pointer: - g5-7 SATISFIED: duplicate test cleanup (lines 76-94 deletion in test_vikunja_client_scope.py) - g5-8 SATISFIED: env-fallback default `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) ...)` in all 6 PS1 scripts - g5-9 SATISFIED: $McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json' in agents-cadence-monitor + la_merge_approve - g5-10 FORWARD to 2.10: A11 anomaly + g5-7/8/9 disposition log + f1 disposition log EA-5 then restates verbatim in iteration-1 gate.

defect.topic: binding_acknowledgments_27_total UNDERCOUNTS the actual binding set
defect.v4_claim: v4 lines 332-344: "binding_acknowledgments_27_total" with explicit listings for i1-1..i1-7 (incorrect range), g4-1..g4-7, g5-1..g5-6, g5-7/8/9 SATISFIED, g5-10 FORWARD, f1 SATISFIED. Implicitly omits 9 critical_carryovers (n=1..n=9) and 3 g3 acks (g3-1, g3-2, g3-3).
defect.actual_count: - 9 critical_carryovers (n=1..n=9) -- OMITTED from v4 - 3 plus_guide_3 (g3-1, g3-2, g3-3) -- OMITTED from v4 - 4 plus_iteration_1 (i1-1, i1-2, i1-3, i1-4) -- v4 says "i1-1..i1-7" which is WRONG (only 4 exist) - 7 plus_guide_4 (g4-1..g4-7) -- correct - 4 plus_guide_5_pre_corrections (g5-1, g5-2, g5-3, g5-4) -- v4 lumps as "g5-1..g5-6" - 6 plus_guide_5_corrections_cycle (g5-5 f1 disposition, g5-6 path-confusion, g5-7 dup tests, g5-8 env-fallback, g5-9 McpConfigPath, g5-10 A11) -- v4 lumps part of these as "g5-1..g5-6" - 1 f1 (treated as separate ack) Total: 9 + 3 + 4 + 7 + 4 + 6 + 1 = 34 acks (or 33 if some dedup)
defect.impact: v4 says "27_total" -- this is approximately the count BEFORE the corrections cycle (9+3+4+7+4 = 27, matching g5-1..g5-4 era). EA-4 forgot to update the count after the g5-5..g5-10 corrections cycle. EA-5 might think only 27 acks bind when ~33-34 do. The original 9 critical_carryovers + 3 g3 acks aren't even mentioned in v4's binding list.
defect.required_correction_for_ea_5_init_pointe: Guide-#6's EA-5 init pointer MUST include the FULL 33-34 ack list with verbatim text from the chain of custody (EA-3 iter-2 gate has the original 9+3+4 = 16 acks verbatim; Guide-#4 added g4-1..g4-7; Guide-#5 init pointer added g5-1..g5-4; Guide-#5 corrections cycle added g5-5..g5-10). Source files for verbatim restatement: - 9 critical_carryovers + 3 g3-N + 4 i1-N: in 2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml - 7 g4-N: in 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml + 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml - 4 g5-1..g5-4: in 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml - 2 g5-5/g5-6: in 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A.xml - 4 g5-7/8/9/10: in 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml EA-5 iteration-1 gate restates all ~33 verbatim with SATISFIED / FORWARD-ACTIVE status.

defect.topic: cleaner.py undersells the g5-3 closure work
defect.v4_claim: v4 lines 112-115: cleaner.py loc_changed="~3" disposition="REFACTORED_minimal" with "Only change: chokepoint routing alignment for downstream f1 fix propagation. Function signatures unchanged."
defect.actual: cleaner.py change was substantive (~44 LOC per git diff --stat at the time): added `from tools import _project_context` import; added `project_context: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg to run_sweep() (function signature DID change); added 3-branch fallback logic (config-injected wins → project_context auto-resolve → legacy zero-arg) replacing single-line fallback at line 180; added detailed docstring explaining Stage 2.4 g5-3 disposition Option (b) rationale. This was the g5-3 closure (Guide-#5 EA-4 init pointer ack) per Option b.
defect.impact: Documentation undersells the work; doesn't credit g5-3 SATISFIED. EA-5 reading v4 might think cleaner.py only had a trivial chokepoint alignment, miss the project-aware fallback design.
defect.required_correction: Note in EA-5 init pointer that cleaner.py closure was substantive g5-3 Option (b) implementation (project-aware fallback with 3-branch logic). Cite Guide-#5 disposition 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_2.4_COMPLETE.xml check id="g5_3_cleaner_closed" verdict="PASS_OPTION_B_THOROUGH" for verbatim disposition documentation.

defect.v4_claim: v4 lines 142-145: "corrected helper signature/routing in tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient to close f1 finding."
defect.actual: The actual f1 fix was: extended `_require_project_id` rejection condition with `or project_id <= 0`; updated docstring to mention "explicit positive int (not <= 0)"; updated error message; added 2 new tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative). The function SIGNATURE was unchanged; only the BODY (rejection condition + error message) and the test file were modified. "Routing" is unrelated to f1 (routing was the o1 concern, separate).
defect.impact: Cosmetic mischaracterization; EA-5 would re-read tools/_vikunja_client.py and see the actual change. No procedural drift.
defect.required_correction: Note in EA-5 init pointer that f1 fix = rejection condition extension + tests, not signature/routing.

defect.actual: Disk verification shows the actual on-disk pattern in the 5 G-1 PS1 scripts is: ``` [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot = $(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' }) ``` WITHOUT the [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)] decorator. EA-4's documentation includes a decorator that doesn't exist in the actual files.
defect.impact: Cosmetic only; the implicit-mandatory-false default applies regardless of explicit [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)] decorator. Functionally equivalent.
defect.required_correction: None required at EA-5 init pointer level. EA-5 will re-read the scripts directly if it needs the pattern.

disposition.decision: NO v4-amend commit. The v4 file persists as-is for audit (it documents what EA-4 knew at v4 emission time -- a snapshot, not a guarantee of perfection). v4-amend would add procedural noise (another mid-stage commit, requires reopening EA-4 chat) without meaningful benefit.

Guide-#6's EA-5 init pointer becomes the AUTHORITATIVE forward statement: corrects g5-N mapping, restates the full ~33 binding ack list verbatim, properly credits g5-3 cleaner.py closure. EA-5 reads init pointer + restates verbatim in iteration-1 gate. The v4 documentation defects don't propagate forward.

For audit completeness, EA-5's eventual 2.10 STATUS append should include a brief footnote noting "v4 handoff documentation contained binding-ack-count drift (claimed 27; actual ~33); corrected in EA-5 iteration-1 gate via Guide-#6 init pointer." This preserves the historical record without polluting v4 itself.

addendum_to_handoff_brief.addendum: This v4 quality review file (2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND.xml) should be added to the EA-5 init pointer's reading list as the 9th Guide-#5 disposition file. Guide-#6 reads this when running Variant B comprehension gate; uses the corrections to author the corrected EA-5 init pointer.

The handoff brief written immediately prior to this file (2.5-GUIDE_HANDOFF_INSTANCE_5_TO_6.xml) enumerates 8 Guide-#5 disposition XMLs in step 2 of first_action_for_successor. Guide-#6 should treat THIS file as the 9th -- not a separate disposition cycle but a quality-review addendum to the v4 handoff itself.

Specifically: after Guide-#6 reads v4 + this quality review, the EA-5 init pointer MUST: (a) Restate g5-7 = duplicate test cleanup (NOT v4's "env-fallback") (b) Restate g5-8 = env-fallback in 6 PS1 (NOT v4's "alias attribute order") (c) Keep g5-9 = McpConfigPath derivation (v4 correct on this one) (d) Keep g5-10 = A11 documented deviation (v4 correct) (e) Restate the FULL ~33 binding ack list (not v4's "27"), drawing verbatim from each source file noted in defect D2 required_correction. (f) Properly credit cleaner.py as g5-3 SATISFIED via Option (b) substantive refactor (NOT v4's "trivial chokepoint alignment").

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: APPROVED_FOR_PROCESS_CONTINUITY -- v4 commit + work-completion claims are sound on disk; documentation defects are forward-correctable in EA-5 init pointer without rolling back v4 or amending. EA-4 may close session; v4 emission stands as the predecessor handoff. LA may proceed with the planned Option B Guide-#5-to-6 transition. Guide-#6 absorbs this quality review during Variant B comprehension gate + corrects in EA-5 init pointer. EA-5 iteration-1 gate becomes the authoritative ack-chain forward statement.
